html:
Exclusive to:

The Stone Fruit is an Item exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in Return of Them. Picking a Stone Fruit Bush yields 3 Stone Fruits at a time, though if left on the bush for too long, the Stone Fruits will appear cracked and picking the bush will yield nothing. Stone Fruits can also fall during Earthquakes in the Lunar Grotto.

Stone Fruits are not edible, but they can be mined to crack them open. Mining a Stone Fruit has a 65% chance to give a Ripe Stone Fruit, a 34% chance for a Rock, or a 1% chance for a Sprouting Stone Fruit. Stone Fruit can also be worked by the Weather Pain, Slurtle Slime, or Gunpowder. Both Slurtle Slime and Gunpowder can mine up to 20 stacks of Stone Fruit at once (20 full stacks of 40 Stone Fruits, i.e. up to 800 total Stone Fruits).

Ripe Stone Fruit

Ripe Stone Fruits have a chance to be obtain by mining Stone Fruits. Unlike their raw counterparts, they can be eaten, restoring 12.5 Hunger and 1 Health. They can be Cooked over Fire to obtain Cooked Stone Fruits which restore slightly more Health. Both versions of Stone Fruit can be used in the Crock Pot as a Vegetable. Ripe Stone Fruits can be used to substitute raw Cactus Flesh in the recipe for Guacamole.

Usage

Fillers cannot be Fruit.

Tips

  • Although Ripe Stone Fruits spoil extremely quickly, unbroken Stone Fruits never spoil and are a very useful emergency food source.
  • Stone Fruits are one of the easiest vegetables to farm for powerful recipes that require vegetables, such as Pierogi, Tall Scotch Eggs, and Beefy Greens. Their hands-off growth cycle makes it easy to amass huge amounts of Stone Fruits with just a handful of Stone Fruit Bushes.

Trivia

  • Stone Fruits were introduced in the Turn of Tides update.
  • While the Stone Fruit's appearance resembles that of real-life avocados, its name plays off the literal meaning of the term "stone fruit," which refers to a broad category of fleshy fruits containing a single hardened pit known as a drupe. Examples include cherries, peaches, and coconuts.
    • This is misleading, as avocados themselves aren't drupes; they are in fact a single-seeded berry.
  • Webber and WX-78 mention needing to "wait for it to ripen". However, time does not affect the Stone Fruit in any way.

“I'd shatter my teeth on that.”
–Wilson, when examining a Stone Fruit.
“I bet it'll taste good when it's ripe.”
–Willow, when examining a Stone Fruit.
“Why little fruit hurt mouth?”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Stone Fruit.
“It grew on a tree, but it looks like a rock.”
–Wendy, when examining a Stone Fruit.
“I DO NOT WANT TO WAIT FOR IT TO RIPEN”
–WX-78, when examining a Stone Fruit.
“The outside is mineral, yet the inside is organic.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Stone Fruit.
“Too hard to eat, but at least it smells nice.”
–Woodie, when examining a Stone Fruit.
“I'd rather not shatter my teeth on that.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Stone Fruit.
“Tis either a röck ör vegetable. Neither are fööd.”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Stone Fruit.
“We have to wait for it to ripen.”
–Webber, when examining a Stone Fruit.
“You can't expect me to eat this!”
–Warly, when examining a Stone Fruit.
“Too crusty”
–Wormwood, when examining a Stone Fruit.
“Not sure how to crack this nut.”
–Winona, when examining a Stone Fruit.
“I don't mean to balk, but that looks like a rock.”
–Wortox, when examining a Stone Fruit.
“Ow! Too hard!”
–Wurt, when examining a Stone Fruit.
“It's hard as a rock!”
–Walter, when examining a Stone Fruit.
“That looks like a quick way to chip a tooth.”
–Wanda, when examining a Stone Fruit.

“Uncooked stone fruit is the pits.”
–Wilson, when examining a Ripe Stone Fruit.
“It's ripe!”
–Willow, when examining a Ripe Stone Fruit.
“Is soft for the eatings now.”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Ripe Stone Fruit.
“I can eat it now.”
–Wendy, when examining a Ripe Stone Fruit.
“IT LOOKS EDIBLE TO ME”
–WX-78, when examining a Ripe Stone Fruit.
“I miss a nice fresh avocado.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Ripe Stone Fruit.
“I think I could sink my teeth into it now.”
–Woodie, when examining a Ripe Stone Fruit.
“It's ripe enough to eat now.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Ripe Stone Fruit.
“Yuck.”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Ripe Stone Fruit.
“We cracked it open.”
–Webber, when examining a Ripe Stone Fruit.
“The fruit seems to have softened somewhat.”
–Warly, when examining a Ripe Stone Fruit.
“Rocks for belly”
–Wormwood, when examining a Ripe Stone Fruit.
“I think I could eat it now.”
–Winona, when examining a Ripe Stone Fruit.
“My mortal friends would find it tasty now.”
–Wortox, when examining a Ripe Stone Fruit.
“It ready!”
–Wurt, when examining a Ripe Stone Fruit.
“It's finally ripe! ...I think.”
–Walter, when examining a Ripe Stone Fruit.
“After all that waiting, it's finally somewhat edible.”
–Wanda, when examining a Ripe Stone Fruit.
“It's actually soft enough to eat now.”
–Wilson, when examining a Cooked Stone Fruit.
“I like it with just a pinch of fire.”
–Willow, when examining a Cooked Stone Fruit.
“Mushy and warm. Yum.”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Cooked Stone Fruit.
“It might actually taste good at this point.”
–Wendy, when examining a Cooked Stone Fruit.
“MY WEAK MINIONS DESIRE MUSHINESS FOR THEIR FLESH STOMACHES”
–WX-78, when examining a Cooked Stone Fruit.
“It's perfectly safe for consumption.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Cooked Stone Fruit.
“Wish I had some toast.”
–Woodie, when examining a Cooked Stone Fruit.
“It looks almost appetizing.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Cooked Stone Fruit.
“I shall nöt eat that.”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Cooked Stone Fruit.
“No more pit!”
–Webber, when examining a Cooked Stone Fruit.
“Cooking really does wonders.”
–Warly, when examining a Cooked Stone Fruit.
“Cooked belly stuff”
–Wormwood, when examining a Cooked Stone Fruit.
“Who knew rocks were so tasty looking inside?”
–Winona, when examining a Cooked Stone Fruit.
“A meal fit for a mortal.”
–Wortox, when examining a Cooked Stone Fruit.
“Made it yummier!”
–Wurt, when examining a Cooked Stone Fruit.
“That's better.”
–Walter, when examining a Cooked Stone Fruit.
“I don't know if it was worth the time to cook it.”
–Wanda, when examining a Cooked Stone Fruit.
Ripe Stone Fruits have a chance to be obtain by mining Stone Fruits. Unlike their raw counterparts, they can be eaten, restoring 12.5 Hunger and 1 Health. They can be Cooked over Fire to obtain Cooked Stone Fruits which restore slightly more Health. Both versions of Stone Fruit can be used in the Crock Pot as a Vegetable. Ripe Stone Fruits can be used to substitute raw Cactus Flesh in the recipe for Guacamole.
Usage[edit | edit source]

Although Ripe Stone Fruits spoil extremely quickly, unbroken Stone Fruits never spoil and are a very useful emergency food source.
Stone Fruits are one of the easiest vegetables to farm for powerful recipes that require vegetables, such as Pierogi, Tall Scotch Eggs, and Beefy Greens. Their hands-off growth cycle makes it easy to amass huge amounts of Stone Fruits with just a handful of Stone Fruit Bushes.

This is misleading, as avocados themselves aren't drupes; they are in fact a single-seeded berry.
Webber and WX-78 mention needing to "wait for it to ripen". However, time does not affect the Stone Fruit in any way.

Their hands-off growth cycle makes it easy to amass huge amounts of Stone Fruits with just a handful of Stone Fruit Bushes. == {{Pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia == * Stone Fruits were introduced in the ''[[Return of Them#Turn of Tides|Turn of Tides]]'' update. * While the Stone Fruit's appearance resembles that of real-life avocados, its name plays off the literal meaning of the term "stone fruit," which refers to a broad category of fleshy fruits containing a single hardened pit known as a drupe. Examples include cherries, peaches, and coconuts. ** This is misleading, as avocados themselves aren't drupes; they are in fact a single-seeded berry. * [[Webber]] and [[WX-78]] mention needing to "wait for it to ripen". However, time does not affect the Stone Fruit in any way.
